description_long: This object consists of two yellow daffodil-like flowers with six broad, pointed petals each, radiating symmetrically around a prominent orange trumpet-shaped corona at the center. The petals are arranged in a single whorl, slightly overlapping at their bases, and display a soft, matte texture. Each flower is supported by a slender, green stem that appears smooth and slightly curved, giving a natural, organic posture. At the base, several narrow, linear green leaves emerge, adding to the botanical realism. The flowers are positioned at different heights, with one slightly higher and more upright than the other, suggesting a natural growth pattern. The overall coloration is dominated by pale yellow petals, orange centers, and green stems and leaves, making it visually striking and easily recognizable as a springtime flower.
description: Two yellow daffodil-like flowers with orange centers, supported by slender green stems and narrow leaves. The petals are broad, pointed, and arranged in a symmetrical whorl.
description_view_0: Front view showing both flowers clearly, with their yellow petals and orange centers fully visible, and green stems and leaves at the base.
description_view_1: A more direct view of one flower facing forward, with the second flower partially obscured behind, highlighting the petal arrangement and central corona.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the thinness of the petals and the length of the stems, with the flowers' profiles and the trumpet-shaped centers visible.
description_view_3: Angled rear view showing the backs of the petals and the stems, with the flowers tilted away and the leaves clustered at the base.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a single whorl, radiating symmetrically around the central corona, with slight overlapping at the base and clear radial symmetry.
